Convolutional network from scratch¶

This is the document for the version of the model build from scratch without using a pretrained model.

In [4]:
import os, shutil, pathlib
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import keras
from keras import layers
from keras import Sequential
from keras.utils import image_dataset_from_directory

original_dir = pathlib.Path("./orig")
new_base_dir = pathlib.Path("./sorted")

Data Preparation¶

The initial part of data preparation including renaming the files, fixing orientation, and creating the sorted datasets is the same in all models, but only shown here.

In [208]:
# suffle files and rename them

import random
for category in ["two", "three"]:
    category_dir = original_dir / category
    # shuffle the files
    files = os.listdir(category_dir)
    random.shuffle(files)
    for i, filename in enumerate(os.listdir(category_dir)):
        old_path = category_dir / filename
        new_name = f"{category}.{i}.jpg"
        new_path = category_dir / new_name
        
        os.rename(old_path, new_path)
In [269]:
# fix image rotation problem importing pictures from windows to linux

from PIL import Image, ImageOps
from pathlib import Path

for category in ["two", "three"]:
    category_dir = original_dir / category
    for path in category_dir.glob("*.jpg"):
        img = Image.open(path)
        img = ImageOps.exif_transpose(img)  # rotates the pictures according to the exif-data
        img.save(path)
In [270]:
# make data subsets

def make_subset(subset_name, start_index, end_index):
    for category in ("two", "three"):
        dir = new_base_dir / subset_name / category
        os.makedirs(dir)
        fnames = [f"{category}.{i}.jpg" for i in range(start_index, end_index)]
        for fname in fnames:
            shutil.copyfile(src=original_dir / category / fname, dst=dir / fname)
            
make_subset("train", start_index=0, end_index = 44)
make_subset("validation", start_index=44, end_index = 54)
make_subset("test", start_index=54, end_index = 64)
In [6]:
# gather datasets
train_dataset = image_dataset_from_directory(
    new_base_dir / "train",
    image_size = (180, 180),
    batch_size = 8)
validation_dataset = image_dataset_from_directory(
    new_base_dir / "validation",
    image_size = (180, 180),
    batch_size = 8)
test_dataset = image_dataset_from_directory(
    new_base_dir / "test",
    image_size = (180, 180),
    batch_size = 8)

Found 88 files belonging to 2 classes.
Found 20 files belonging to 2 classes.
Found 20 files belonging to 2 classes.
In [7]:
data_augmentation = keras.Sequential(
    [
        layers.RandomFlip("horizontal"),
        layers.RandomRotation(0.05),
        layers.RandomZoom(0.1)
    ]
)
In [8]:
# examples of data augmentation
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))
for images, _ in train_dataset.take(1):
    for i in range(9):
        augmented_images = data_augmentation(images, training=True)
        ax = plt.subplot(3, 3, i+1)
        plt.imshow(augmented_images[0].numpy().astype("uint8"))
        plt.axis("off")

2026-04-15 09:14:11.554926: I tensorflow/core/framework/local_rendezvous.cc:407] Local rendezvous is aborting with status: OUT_OF_RANGE: End of sequence

Model¶

In [9]:
# build model
model = Sequential([ 
    layers.Input(shape=(180, 180, 3)),
    data_augmentation,
    layers.Rescaling(scale=1./255),
    layers.Conv2D(32, (3, 3), activation='relu'), 
    layers.MaxPooling2D((2, 2)),  
    layers.Conv2D(64, (3, 3), activation='relu'),
    layers.MaxPooling2D((2, 2)),
    layers.Flatten(), 
    layers.Dropout(0.4),
    layers.Dense(1, activation='sigmoid') ]) 

model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='binary_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])
In [10]:
history = model.fit(train_dataset, validation_data=validation_dataset, epochs=50)

In [11]:
# print validation loss/accuracy
test_scores = model.evaluate(validation_dataset, verbose=2)
print("Validation loss:", test_scores[0])
print("Validation accuracy:", test_scores[1])

# Plot validation loss and accuracy compared to training loss and accuracy
accuracy = history.history["accuracy"]
val_accuracy = history.history["val_accuracy"]
loss = history.history["loss"]
val_loss = history.history["val_loss"]
epochs = list(range(1, len(accuracy)+1))
plt.plot(epochs, accuracy, label="Training accuracy")
plt.plot(epochs, val_accuracy, label="Validation accuracy")
plt.legend()
plt.figure()

plt.plot(history.history['loss'], label='train loss')
plt.plot(history.history['val_loss'], label='val loss')
plt.legend()
plt.xlabel('Epoch')
plt.ylabel('Loss')
